label: Iran Killed
contestNote: Iran claims 1,300+ civilians killed and ~10,000 civilian sites struck; Hengaw estimates 4,300+ total (incl. ~390 civilians) in first 10 days; HRANA estimates exceed 2,400 including 181+ children; internet blackout (~1% connectivity / 240hrs) severely limits verification. 5,000+ targets struck by US/Israel per CENTCOM.

label: Israel Killed
deltaNote: 2 civilians killed by Iranian cluster warhead missile in Yehud on March 10. Over 2,142 Israelis wounded in Iranian missile and drone attacks.

label: Gulf Region Killed
deltaNote: Includes UAE (5, inc. 2 military), Bahrain (1), Kuwait (2), Saudi Arabia (2 foreign nationals), Oman (1). Gulf states reporting first direct civilian fatalities from Iranian strikes.

label: Lebanon Killed
source: Lebanese health authorities / Lebanese gov disaster office
deltaNote: Death toll includes 83+ children. 750,000 displaced. French emergency UN Security Council meeting called re: Lebanon.

label: Brent Crude
deltaNote: Wild swings March 11: plunged 17% to below $80 on ceasefire speculation and IEA reserve release reports, then rebounded to ~$90 after US Energy Secretary Chris Wright briefly posted (then deleted) a claim of Navy tanker escort through Hormuz. IEA considering largest-ever strategic reserve release.

label: Flights Cancelled
deltaNote: Dubai International Airport attacked by Iranian drones. Israel suspended all civilian air travel. UAE extended flight disruptions.

label: Countries Affected
deltaNote: Iran, Israel, Lebanon, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Oman, Iraq, Jordan, Azerbaijan, Turkey, Cyprus (UK RAF base). Pakistan also affected (consulate attack).
